pi_items.source: Verified via setup_task.sh - synthetic product created with name 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' at $24.99 regular price. Realistic ecommerce product name.
Verified as valid WooCommerce tax class name format from documentation. Custom tax classes can use any descriptive name in capitalized format.
Verified via WooCommerce documentation: single-word tax class names are slugified to lowercase. 'Apparel' -> 'apparel' (matches export_result.sh query which uses 'WHERE tax_rate_class = 'apparel'').
Verified via WooCommerce documentation: tax rates are stored with 4 decimal places format (e.g., 4.0000 for 4%). This is the standard WooCommerce tax rate format.
Verified as standard two-letter postal abbreviation for New York state (US postal service standard). ISO 3166-2:US compliant.
Verified as standard ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for United States. Used in WooCommerce tax configuration.
Verified via export_result.sh code structure which queries 'tax_rate_name' column. User-defined descriptive name following standard pattern (location + type). Valid and reasonable format.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified and correct. The task uses synthetic data: a product 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' created during setup at $24.99. Tax configuration parameters are accurate: tax class 'Apparel' will be slugified to 'apparel'; tax rate format is 4.0000 (4%); state/country codes are NY and US respectively; tax name 'NY Apparel Tax' follows standard naming conventions. All values are internally consistent with WooCommerce specifications.
